A.rationale: Correct code, leaner docstring but no Args/Returns; longest narrative tail (failure-modes section + question) is narration beyond the ask.

B.rationale: Correct, full Args/Returns/Raises docstring, {n!r}; tightest output with brief edge note and one question. Best signal per token.

C.rationale: Same strong docstring and guard as B; edge-case list adds slight narration, marginally longer but well-organized and accurate.

D.rationale: Adds isinstance(n,int) beyond the n<=0 ask; rejects valid int-like types (numpy ints) and broadens documented behavior. Otherwise clean and concise.

notes: All four share the correct one-line comprehension. B wins: complete docstring, repr in error, tightest tail. C ties on quality but slightly wordier. A's docstring lacks Args/Returns and has the longest narration. D's isinstance check overreaches the spec.
